sourceMovie.title: Broken Arrow
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Broken Arrow (1996) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. John Woo's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Predator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Broken Arrow clicked for you, Predator (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Broken Arrow?
faq.answer: Try Predator, Die Hard: With a Vengeance, Die Hard 2—each shares DNA with Broken Arrow while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Broken Arrow?
faq.answer: Broken Arrow (1996) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Broken Arrow worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
